channel.title: The Sentinel Review — National Security Feed
channel.description: Automated national security news aggregation. Updated every 8 hours.

item.title: 📍 What if the A-10 had AI & electronic-warfare gear? | Defense One
item.description: House lawmakers wonder whether new missions could help keep the Warthog useful.

item.title: 🏛️ VC-25B Air Force One “Bridge” Aircraft Now Wears Trump’s Preferred Red, White, and Blue Paint Job | The War Zone
item.description: The VC-25B "Bridge" jet is also undergoing a final round of modifications before its official debut this summer. The post VC-25B Air Force One “Bridge” Aircraft Now Wears Trump’s Preferred Red, White, and Blue Paint Job appeared first on The War Zone.

item.title: 🏴 The Ukraine Support Act sends a strong signal even if it won’t send weapons | Atlantic Council
item.description: As momentum in the Russia-Ukraine War shifts in Kyiv’s favor, the US Congress took a tentative step last week toward rewarding Ukraine’s progress with much-needed weapons for their troops and support for the country, writes Leslie Shedd. The post The Ukraine Support Act sends a…

item.title: 🌏 Australia should take interest in Takaichi’s efforts to return abductees from North Korea | ASPI Strategist
item.description: With the sole surviving parent of North Korea’s Japanese abductees turning 90 this year, Japanese Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi is facing domestic pressure to act. She wants direct talks with North Korean leader Kim Jong-un ...

item.title: 🌏 Japan’s Security Focus Shift | The Diplomat
item.description: China’s reach is extending beyond the First Island Chain. Japan is taking note.

item.title: 🌍 Iran, Israel Pull Back From Brink After Trading Fire | Foreign Policy
item.description: But another Israeli assault on Beirut could collapse the two sides’ fragile truce.

item.title: 📍 Iwo Jima Completes Deployment, Nimitz Heading Back To The United States | The War Zone
item.description: Here's where America's aircraft carriers and big-deck amphibious assault ships are as of June 8, 2026. The post Iwo Jima Completes Deployment, Nimitz Heading Back To The United States appeared first on The War Zone.

item.title: 🌍 Trump Started a War He Can’t Control | Foreign Policy
item.description: He wants to end the Iran war. But Iran, Israel, and Hezbollah have other ideas.

item.title: 🏴 Franco-German Future Fighter Effort Collapses Over Irreconcilable Differences | The War Zone
item.description: Germany and France failed to come to terms on requirements, apparently bringing Europe’s flagship next gen fighter program to an end. The post Franco-German Future Fighter Effort Collapses Over Irreconcilable Differences appeared first on The War Zone.

item.title: 🌏 In minerals purchasing, China is teaching Australia a lesson on economic power | ASPI Strategist
item.description: For decades, Australia assumed economic power flowed from geology. Resource wealth delivered export earnings, government revenue and strategic confidence. The emergence of China Mineral Resources Group (CMRG) over the past four years suggests otherwise. In ...

item.title: 📍 KC-135 Tanker Spotted With New Satellite Communications Antenna | The War Zone
item.description: The USAF says improved connectivity is critical for the survivability and effectiveness of the KC-135 fleet. The post KC-135 Tanker Spotted With New Satellite Communications Antenna appeared first on The War Zone.

item.title: 📍 What to expect at the Berlin Air Show | Breaking Defense
item.description: Aaron Mehta and Tim Martin walk through what they’re looking for at the 2026 Berlin Air Show.

item.title: 📍 2026 Global Energy Agenda full survey results | Atlantic Council
item.description: The Global Energy Agenda takes the pulse of the energy community, revealing expert views on global energy security, access, and sustainability. The post 2026 Global Energy Agenda full survey results appeared first on Atlantic Council.

item.title: 📍 How defense teams can scale AI without increasing data risk | Breaking Defense
item.description: [Sponsored] AI is expanding the mission data footprint across defense environments, increasing the need for scalable, layered data-at-rest (DAR) protection.

item.title: 🏛️ Trump memo on AI aims to avoid repeat of Anthropic debacle | Breaking Defense
item.description: The “National Security Presidential Memorandum” urges closer collaboration with AI companies — as long as they’re compliant with Pentagon demands — and orders a sweeping revision of Biden-era guardrails on military AI.

item.title: 🌍 Iran And Israel Step Back From The Brink | The War Zone
item.description: After a flurry of tit-for-tat strikes, Israel and Iran pulled back from entering into yet another all-out war. The post Iran And Israel Step Back From The Brink appeared first on The War Zone.

item.title: 🌏 Lee Jae-myung’s First Year Successes Put South Korea on the Path to Tangible Results | The Diplomat
item.description: Last year, Lee stated in his inauguration speech that pragmatism and the national interest were the guiding principles of his presidency.

item.title: 🏴 Airbus Unveils U145 Uncrewed Cargo Helicopter | The War Zone
item.description: The U145 transforms one of Europe’s most successful helicopters into an uncrewed aircraft optimized for risky cargo missions. The post Airbus Unveils U145 Uncrewed Cargo Helicopter appeared first on The War Zone.

item.title: 🌍 US approves Kuwait request to buy nearly $2 billion of counter-drone platforms | Defense News
item.description: Following recent Iranian strikes on Kuwait, the U.S. approved a potential foreign military sale of counter-UAS platforms made by Anduril.

item.title: 🏴 Is Skepticism Ukraine’s Foreign Policy Playbook? | War on the Rocks
item.description: Welcome to The Ukraine Compass, a weekly digest of Ukrainian commentary and analysis from across the political spectrum only for War on the Rocks members. Each Monday, we bring you a curated selection of articles from Ukrainian media offering insight into how Ukrainians…

item.title: 📍 Franco-German-Spanish FCAS fighter program dead: Reports | Breaking Defense
item.description: The German Chancellor, Friedrich Merz, would have proposed that the two nations, along with Spain, which is also involved as a partner, continue to jointly develop the so-called combat cloud communication network.

item.title: 🏛️ US Navy F/A-18 Super Hornet strikes, disables oil tanker in Gulf of Oman | Defense News
item.description: An F/A-18 Super Hornet assigned to the aircraft carrier USS Abraham Lincoln fired a precision munition into the vessel's engineering and steering spaces.

item.title: 🌍 Show Us the Money | Foreign Policy
item.description: How satire, social media, and shame are forging Syria’s new public square.

item.title: 🌏 China Is Providing AI That’s Literate in Africa’s Languages | Foreign Policy
item.description: Chinese models have become the overwhelming choice for African developers.

item.title: 🏴 Germany and France drop joint fighter jet project | Defense News
item.description: Failure to reach an agreement underscores the struggles Europe has faced in rebuilding its military capacity after decades of underinvestment.

item.title: 🌏 State Department approves FMS agreements with New Zealand, South Korea | Breaking Defense
item.description: New Zealand could receive five Lockheed Martin MH-60R Seahawk helicopters as well as airborne low-frequency sonars.

item.title: 🏴 US must learn lessons from Ukraine, innovate faster and cheaper: Anduril president | Defense News
item.description: Anduril President Christian Brose discussed the need to develop cheaper weapons systems at scale to avoid quick depletion of exquisite munitions.

item.title: 📍 Litigation Tracker: Legal Challenges to Trump Administration Actions | Just Security
item.description: A public resource tracking all the legal challenges to the Trump administration's executive orders and actions. The post Litigation Tracker: Legal Challenges to Trump Administration Actions appeared first on Just Security.

item.title: 🏴 Video Captures Rafale Fighter’s Drone Kill Over Baltic | The War Zone
item.description: What appears to be the first footage of an engagement of this kind in Europe underscores the growing challenge drones pose to NATO air policing. The post Video Captures Rafale Fighter’s Drone Kill Over Baltic appeared first on The War Zone.

item.title: 📍 Polish-Ukrainian startup develops radar to track elusive, low-flying drones | Defense News
item.description: The technology aims to find small, low-flying drones, including those connected to operators via long, fiber-optic command wires.
